topic: Theme 2: Political and Artistic Culture in the Spanish-speaking World
subject: spanish

items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').

items.q: Why does 'qué' have an accent in '¿Qué quieres?' but not always elsewhere?
items.correct: The accent distinguishes interrogative/exclamative 'qué' from relative 'que'.
items.why: Students see two spellings of the 'same word'. Spanish uses written accents to distinguish homophones with different functions: qué (question/exclamation) vs que (that/which - relative).

items.q: How do you say 'I study English at school' in Spanish?
items.misconception: Estudio el inglés a la escuela.
items.why: Students add unnecessary articles or wrong prepositions. Languages as school subjects DROP the article: 'estudio inglés', 'me gusta el inglés' (general likes need article). Location: EN (not a) for places: en el colegio, en casa, en clase.

items.q: How many diputados sit in the Congreso?
items.why: It's 350. “230” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is the future of 'votar' for 'él'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: votará. “votó” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was the current Spanish Constitution adopted?
items.why: Exact answer: 1978. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
